_description: Shadow correlation attack — an adversary observing multiple dispatches of identical content across a channel boundary attempts to correlate shadow_ids to reconstruct content provenance. This is structurally prevented by Photophore's SHADOW-06 no-caching guarantee: each call to shadow.generate() with IDENTICAL (content, content_type) inputs produces a DISTINCT shadow_id (fresh UUIDv4 per dispatch). An implementation caching shadows via @lru_cache, @functools.cache, or a module-level dict is non-compliant.
_behavioral_wire: photophore/python/tests/test_shadow_uniqueness_property.py (Hypothesis: >=100 cases x 100 inner calls, all shadow_ids distinct) and photophore/python/tests/test_shadow_no_caching.py (grep gate + behavioral)

scenario.description: An attacker submits the same tier-1 content block in 100 consecutive dispatches through the same channel and observes the shadow_ids in the outbound envelopes.

shadow.abstraction: document of length class short, topic category general, temporal current

scenario.expected_property: 100 calls to shadow.generate(content, content_type) with IDENTICAL (content, content_type) produce 100 DISTINCT shadow_ids. Verified by Hypothesis property test (test_shadow_uniqueness_property.py).
scenario.violation_pattern: An implementation caching shadows (e.g., via @lru_cache keyed on (content, content_type)) returns the SAME shadow_id for identical inputs, enabling cross-dispatch correlation.

expected_behavior: Non-compliant: any implementation that caches shadow results by (content, content_type) key — whether via @lru_cache, @functools.cache, a module-level dict, or any memoization — MUST be rejected. The shadow_id MUST be a fresh UUIDv4 on every call to generate().
notes: Behaviorally wired in Photophore via: (1) Hypothesis property test test_shadow_uniqueness_property.py (>= 100 outer cases x 100 inner generate() calls with identical inputs = >= 10,000 total calls, all shadow_ids distinct); (2) grep gate in test_shadow_no_caching.py (asserts zero @lru_cache/@functools.cache/@cache/= _shadow_cache in photophore.shadow). The dispatch coordinator ensures no Shadow object is retained after dispatch completes.
